content.Text:
[Tue 2026-04-21 14:58 CDT] Run the TAS ad intelligence scraper against two competitor dealerships in Baton Rouge. The scraper is already built and working at ~/.openclaw/workspace/tas/ad-scraper/

## Run these two dealers:

```bash
cd ~/.openclaw/workspace/tas/ad-scraper

# Price LeBlanc Lexus
/opt/homebrew/bin/python3 run_recon.py "Price LeBlanc Lexus"
/opt/homebrew/bin/python3 google_ads.py "priceleblanclexus.com"

# Mercedes-Benz of Baton Rouge  
/opt/homebrew/bin/python3 run_recon.py "Mercedes-Benz of Baton Rouge"
/opt/homebrew/bin/python3 google_ads.py "mbobr.com"
```

## After each run, copy screenshots to workspace:
```bash
cp /tmp/google_ads_*.png ~/.openclaw/workspace/tas/ 2>/dev/null
cp /tmp/meta_ads_*.png ~/.openclaw/workspace/tas/ 2>/dev/null
```

## Important — the Google scraper needs to click the autocomplete suggestion
The fix from the previous session: after typing the dealer name, wait for autocomplete and click the first suggestion rather than pressing Enter. Make sure that logic is still in place. If it falls back to pressing Enter and gets no results, also try the domain-based search (priceleblanclexus.com / mbobr.com).

## Report back with:
1. How many Google ads found for each dealer?
2. Who is running their ads (what agency shows up)?
3. What models/offers are they pushing?
4. How many Meta ads found?
5. Screenshots saved — list them

This is competitive intelligence for Brian Harris BMW. We want to see everything Price LeBlanc Lexus and Mercedes-Benz of Baton Rouge are running on Google and Meta.

Thinking.text: The user wants me to run their existing ad intelligence scraper against two competitor dealerships. Let me first check the scraper code to make sure the autocomplete fix is in place, then run the commands.

content.Text: Let me first check the scraper code for the autocomplete fix, then run everything.

Thinking.text:
I see an issue with the google_ads.py autocomplete logic. The suggestion selectors include a hardcoded `'text=brianharrisbmw.com'` which is specific to Brian Harris BMW. For other dealers, this won't work. The other selectors should handle it though.

Let me also check the meta_ads.py scraper, then run everything. But first, let me fix the hardcoded selector issue - actually, the other generic selectors should work fine as fallbacks. The hardcoded one just won't match and will be skipped.

Actually, the user said "Make sure that logic is still in place" regarding clicking autocomplete suggestions. 

The fallback logic is intact - it cycles through multiple selectors and uses keyboard navigation as a last resort, so the hardcoded domain selector being skipped for other dealers isn't a problem. For domain-based searches, I need to run google_ads.py separately with the domain parameter as specified. Let me quickly verify meta_ads.py and then execute the scrapers.

content.Text: The autocomplete click logic is in place (lines 64-90 of google_ads.py). The hardcoded `brianharrisbmw.com` selector on line 66 won't match for these dealers but the generic selectors (`[role="option"]`, `mat-option`, etc.) will handle it. Let me check meta_ads.py too, then run everything.

Good, everything looks intact. Now let me run both dealers. I'll run them sequentially since they use the same browser resources.

**Starting Price LeBlanc Lexus...**

ToolUse.name: cd ~/.openclaw/workspace/tas/ad-scraper && /opt/homebrew/bin/python3 run_recon.py "Price LeBlanc Lexus" 2>&1
ToolUse.raw_input: {"command":"cd ~/.openclaw/workspace/tas/ad-scraper && /opt/homebrew/bin/python3 run_recon.py \"Price LeBlanc Lexus\" 2>&1","description":"Run full ad recon for Price LeBlanc Lexus","timeout":180000}

input.description: Run full ad recon for Price LeBlanc Lexus

Thinking.text: Google found 0 ads but clicked a suggestion. Let me check the screenshot and also run the domain-based Google search. Let me also run the separate google_ads.py with the domain.

content.Text: Google Ads found 0 for Price LeBlanc via name search (even though it clicked a suggestion). Let me run the domain-based search and check the screenshots.

ToolUse.name: cd ~/.openclaw/workspace/tas/ad-scraper && /opt/homebrew/bin/python3 google_ads.py "priceleblanclexus.com" 2>&1
ToolUse.raw_input: {"command":"cd ~/.openclaw/workspace/tas/ad-scraper && /opt/homebrew/bin/python3 google_ads.py \"priceleblanclexus.com\" 2>&1","description":"Google Ads domain search for Price LeBlanc","timeout":120000}

input.description: Google Ads domain search for Price LeBlanc

Thinking.text:
The Google Ads screenshot shows the search stayed on the landing page - it typed "Price LeBlanc Lexus" but the search didn't execute (no results loaded). The autocomplete click with `[class*="suggestion"]` may have clicked something wrong. The domain search also failed similarly.

The Meta screenshot shows ~4 results with active ads from Price LeBlanc Lexus. The scraper reported 258 ad_count which seems like it picked up too many divs - the screenshot shows ~4 results.

Let me check the actual data file to see what was captured, then move on to Mercedes-Benz. 

I need to review the Google Ads domain search screenshot and the saved data before running the Mercedes-Benz scrape.

content.Text: The Google Ads search isn't executing — the screenshot shows it typed the name but stayed on the landing page. The suggestion click didn't actually trigger a search. Meta found ~4 results though. Let me check the domain search screenshot and the saved data, then run Mercedes-Benz.

Thinking.text:
OK so:

1. Google Ads: Both name and domain search failed - the search typed text but never executed the search. It's staying on the landing page. The `[class*="suggestion"]` click probably clicked something that wasn't actually a suggestion, or the suggestion didn't trigger navigation.

2. Meta Ads: The screenshot shows ~4 results, but the scraper reported 258 ad_count because the `div[class*="x1qjc9v5"]` selector matched 258 random divs (including country list items). The page_text_sample has the real data though - ~4 active ads.

From the Meta page text I can extract the actual ad content:


- Ad 1: "Turn every drive into a statement. Discover bold design and smooth performance in a new Lexus at Price LeBlanc Lexus. Shop the lineup and book your test drive today." - New 2026 Lexus TX 350 PREMIUM AWD
- Ad 2: Same copy, same model - multiple versions
- Ad 3: "Discover refined driving with our hand-selected pre-owned lineup, where quality meets value." - Pre-Owned 2019 Audi Q7 quattro Premium Plus

The Google Ads scraper needs fixing - the search isn't being submitted. After typing and clicking what should be a suggestion, the page stays on the landing page instead of navigating to results.

The issue is likely that the autocomplete dropdown selector isn't targeting the right element, so the click isn't actually triggering the search. I'll move on to scraping Mercedes-Benz data and come back to debug this later.

content.Text:
Google Ads scraper is broken — the search types but never submits. Both name and domain stay on the landing page. The `[class*="suggestion"]` selector is matching a non-functional element. Meta is working but the ad_count (258) is inflated — the real count from the page is ~4 ads.

Let me run Mercedes-Benz now, then I'll address the Google Ads issue.

ToolUse.name: cd ~/.openclaw/workspace/tas/ad-scraper && /opt/homebrew/bin/python3 run_recon.py "Mercedes-Benz of Baton Rouge" 2>&1

1	#!/opt/homebrew/bin/python3
2	"""
3	Google Ads Transparency scraper for TAS.
4	Usage: python3 google_ads.py "Brian Harris BMW"
5	"""
6	
7	import sys
8	import json
9	import time
10	from datetime import datetime
11	from camoufox.sync_api import Camoufox
12	
13	def scrape_google_ads(dealer_name: str, output_file: str = None) -> dict:
14	    results = {
15	        "dealer": dealer_name,
16	        "scraped_at": datetime.now().isoformat(),
17	        "source": "google_ads_transparency",
18	        "active_ads": [],
19	        "ad_count": 0,
20	        "error": None
21	    }
22	
23	    url = f"https://adstransparency.google.com/?q={dealer_name.replace(' ', '+')}&region=US"
24	
25	    try:
26	        with Camoufox(headless=True, geoip=True) as browser:
27	            page = browser.new_page()
28	            page.goto(url, wait_until="networkidle", timeout=30000)
29	            time.sleep(4)
30	
31	            # Take initial screenshot
32	            page.screenshot(path=f"/tmp/google_ads_{dealer_name.replace(' ', '_')}_initial.png")
33	
34	            # Try to interact with search box in case URL params didn't trigger search
35	            page.wait_for_selector('input', timeout=10000)
36	
37	            search_selectors = [
38	                'input[type="search"]',
39	                'input[aria-label*="search" i]',
40	                'input[placeholder*="search" i]',
41	                'input[placeholder*="advertiser" i]',
42	                'mat-search-bar input',
43	                'input.mat-input-element',
44	                'input',  # fallback
45	            ]
46	
47	            search_input = None
48	            for selector in search_selectors:
49	                try:
50	                    el = page.query_selector(selector)
51	                    if el:
52	                        search_input = el
53	                        print(f"Found search input with: {selector}")
54	                        break
55	                except:
56	                    pass
57	
58	            if search_input:
59	                search_input.click()
60	                time.sleep(0.5)
61	                search_input.fill(dealer_name)
62	                time.sleep(2)  # wait for autocomplete dropdown
63	
64	                # Try to click the first autocomplete suggestion
65	                suggestion_selectors = [
66	                    'text=brianharrisbmw.com',
67	                    '[class*="suggestion"]',
68	                    '[class*="autocomplete"] >> visible=true >> nth=0',
69	                    '[role="option"]',
70	                    '[role="listbox"] >> visible=true >> nth=0',
71	                    'mat-option',
72	                ]
73	                clicked_suggestion = False
74	                for sel in suggestion_selectors:
75	                    try:
76	                        el = page.query_selector(sel)
77	                        if el and el.is_visible():
78	                            el.click()
79	                            clicked_suggestion = True
80	                            print(f"Clicked suggestion with: {sel}")
81	                            break
82	                    except:
83	                        pass
84	
85	                if not clicked_suggestion:
86	                    # Fallback: press Enter or ArrowDown+Enter
87	                    print("No suggestion found, trying keyboard navigation")
88	                    page.keyboard.press('ArrowDown')
89	                    time.sleep(0.3)
90	                    page.keyboard.press('Enter')
91	
92	                time.sleep(5)  # wait for results to load
93	                page.screenshot(path=f"/tmp/google_ads_{dealer_name.replace(' ', '_')}_results.png")
94	                content = page.inner_text("body")
95	                results["page_text_sample"] = content[:3000]
96	            else:
97	                results["note"] = "Could not find search input"
98	                content = page.inner_text("body")
99	                results["page_text...

content.Text:
1	#!/opt/homebrew/bin/python3
2	"""
3	TAS Ad Recon — run full ad intelligence for a dealer.
4	Usage: python3 run_recon.py "Brian Harris BMW"
5	Output: tas/data/{dealer_slug}/ads_{date}.json
6	"""
7	
8	import sys
9	import json
10	import os
11	import subprocess
12	import tempfile
13	from datetime import datetime
14	
15	def run_scraper(script_name: str, dealer_name: str) -> dict:
16	    """Run a scraper as a subprocess to avoid asyncio loop conflicts."""
17	    script_dir = os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(__file__))
18	    script_path = os.path.join(script_dir, script_name)
19	
20	    with tempfile.NamedTemporaryFile(mode='w', suffix='.json', delete=False) as f:
21	        tmp_path = f.name
22	
23	    try:
24	        result = subprocess.run(
25	            ["/opt/homebrew/bin/python3", script_path, dealer_name, tmp_path],
26	            capture_output=True, text=True, timeout=60
27	        )
28	        if result.stdout:
29	            print(result.stdout)
30	        if result.stderr:
31	            print(result.stderr, file=sys.stderr)
32	
33	        with open(tmp_path) as f:
34	            return json.load(f)
35	    except subprocess.TimeoutExpired:
36	        return {"dealer": dealer_name, "error": "Scraper timed out after 60s", "ad_count": 0, "active_ads": []}
37	    except Exception as e:
38	        return {"dealer": dealer_name, "error": str(e), "ad_count": 0, "active_ads": []}
39	    finally:
40	        if os.path.exists(tmp_path):
41	            os.unlink(tmp_path)
42	
43	def run_recon(dealer_name: str):
44	    slug = dealer_name.lower().replace(" ", "-")
45	    date = datetime.now().strftime("%Y-%m-%d")
46	
47	    data_dir = os.path.join(os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(__file__)), "..", "data", slug)
48	    os.makedirs(data_dir, exist_ok=True)
49	
50	    output_path = os.path.join(data_dir, f"ads_{date}.json")
51	
52	    print(f"\n🔍 TAS Ad Recon: {dealer_name}")
53	    print(f"{'='*50}")
54	
55	    print("\n📘 Scraping Meta Ad Library...")
56	    meta = run_scraper("meta_ads.py", dealer_name)
57	
58	    print("\n🔍 Scraping Google Ads Transparency...")
59	    google = run_scraper("google_ads.py", dealer_name)
60	
61	    combined = {
62	        "dealer": dealer_name,
63	        "date": date,
64	        "meta": meta,
65	        "google": google,
66	        "summary": {
67	            "meta_ad_count": meta["ad_count"],
68	            "google_ad_count": google["ad_count"],
69	            "meta_error": meta.get("error"),
70	            "google_error": google.get("error"),
71	        }
72	    }
73	
74	    with open(output_path, "w") as f:
75	        json.dump(combined, f, indent=2)
76	
77	    print(f"\n✅ Done. Saved to {output_path}")
78	    print(f"   Meta ads found: {meta['ad_count']}")
79	    print(f"   Google ads found: {google['ad_count']}")
80	
81	    if meta.get("note"): print(f"   Meta note: {meta['note']}")
82	    if google.get("note"): print(f"   Google note: {google['note']}")
83	
84	    print(f"\n📸 Screenshots: /tmp/meta_ads_*.png and /tmp/google_ads_*.png")
85	
86	    return combined
87	
88	
89	if __name__ == "__main__":
90	    dealer = sys.argv[1] if len(sys.argv) > 1 else "Brian Harris BMW"
91	    run_recon(dealer)
92

content.Text:
1	#!/opt/homebrew/bin/python3
2	"""
3	Meta Ad Library scraper for TAS.
4	Pulls active ads for a dealer by name.
5	Usage: python3 meta_ads.py "Brian Harris BMW"
6	"""
7	
8	import sys
9	import json
10	import time
11	from datetime import datetime
12	from camoufox.sync_api import Camoufox
13	
14	def scrape_meta_ads(dealer_name: str, output_file: str = None) -> dict:
15	    """
16	    Scrape Meta Ad Library for a dealer.
17	    Returns dict with ad count, active ads, offers found.
18	    """
19	    results = {
20	        "dealer": dealer_name,
21	        "scraped_at": datetime.now().isoformat(),
22	        "source": "meta_ad_library",
23	        "active_ads": [],
24	        "ad_count": 0,
25	        "error": None
26	    }
27	
28	    url = f"https://www.facebook.com/ads/library/?active_status=active&ad_type=all&country=US&q={dealer_name.replace(' ', '+')}&search_type=keyword_unordered"
29	
30	    try:
31	        with Camoufox(headless=True, geoip=True) as browser:
32	            page = browser.new_page()
33	            page.goto(url, wait_until="networkidle", timeout=30000)
34	            time.sleep(3)  # let JS render
35	
36	            # Take screenshot for debugging
37	            page.screenshot(path=f"/tmp/meta_ads_{dealer_name.replace(' ', '_')}.png")
38	
39	            # Get full page text
40	            content = page.inner_text("body")
41	
42	            # Try to find ad containers
43	            # Meta Ad Library renders ads in divs — look for ad cards
44	            ad_cards = page.query_selector_all('[data-testid="ad-archive-renderer"]')
45	            if not ad_cards:
46	                # Try alternate selectors
47	                ad_cards = page.query_selector_all('div[class*="x1qjc9v5"]')  # Meta's internal class
48	
49	            results["ad_count"] = len(ad_cards)
50	            results["page_text_sample"] = content[:2000]  # first 2000 chars for debugging
51	
52	            # Extract ad text from each card
53	            for i, card in enumerate(ad_cards[:10]):  # limit to 10
54	                try:
55	                    text = card.inner_text()
56	                    results["active_ads"].append({
57	                        "index": i,
58	                        "text": text[:500]
59	                    })
60	                except:
61	                    pass
62	
63	            # If no cards found via selector, parse text for ad indicators
64	            if results["ad_count"] == 0:
65	                # Look for patterns in page text
66	                if "No ads found" in content:
67	                    results["note"] = "No active ads found for this dealer"
68	                elif "Active" in content and ("results" in content.lower() or "ads" in content.lower()):
69	                    results["note"] = "Ads may be present but selectors need updating — check screenshot"
70	                else:
71	                    results["note"] = "Page loaded but no ad data extracted — check screenshot"
72	
73	    except Exception as e:
74	        results["error"] = str(e)
75	
76	    if output_file:
77	        with open(output_file, "w") as f:
78	            json.dump(results, f, indent=2)
79	        print(f"Saved to {output_file}")
80	    else:
81	        print(json.dumps(results, indent=2))
82	
83	    return results
84	
85	
86	if __name__ == "__main__":
87	    dealer = sys.argv[1] if len(sys.argv) > 1 else "Brian Harris BMW"
88	    output = sys.argv[2] if len(sys.argv) > 2 else None
89	    scrape_meta_ads(dealer, output)
90

toolu_01WKNQ5Ks9ER127kmZYjMX25.output:
1	#!/opt/homebrew/bin/python3
2	"""
3	Meta Ad Library scraper for TAS.
4	Pulls active ads for a dealer by name.
5	Usage: python3 meta_ads.py "Brian Harris BMW"
6	"""
7	
8	import sys
9	import json
10	import time
11	from datetime import datetime
12	from camoufox.sync_api import Camoufox
13	
14	def scrape_meta_ads(dealer_name: str, output_file: str = None) -> dict:
15	    """
16	    Scrape Meta Ad Library for a dealer.
17	    Returns dict with ad count, active ads, offers found.
18	    """
19	    results = {
20	        "dealer": dealer_name,
21	        "scraped_at": datetime.now().isoformat(),
22	        "source": "meta_ad_library",
23	        "active_ads": [],
24	        "ad_count": 0,
25	        "error": None
26	    }
27	
28	    url = f"https://www.facebook.com/ads/library/?active_status=active&ad_type=all&country=US&q={dealer_name.replace(' ', '+')}&search_type=keyword_unordered"
29	
30	    try:
31	        with Camoufox(headless=True, geoip=True) as browser:
32	            page = browser.new_page()
33	            page.goto(url, wait_until="networkidle", timeout=30000)
34	            time.sleep(3)  # let JS render
35	
36	            # Take screenshot for debugging
37	            page.screenshot(path=f"/tmp/meta_ads_{dealer_name.replace(' ', '_')}.png")
38	
39	            # Get full page text
40	            content = page.inner_text("body")
41	
42	            # Try to find ad containers
43	            # Meta Ad Library renders ads in divs — look for ad cards
44	            ad_cards = page.query_selector_all('[data-testid="ad-archive-renderer"]')
45	            if not ad_cards:
46	                # Try alternate selectors
47	                ad_cards = page.query_selector_all('div[class*="x1qjc9v5"]')  # Meta's internal class
48	
49	            results["ad_count"] = len(ad_cards)
50	            results["page_text_sample"] = content[:2000]  # first 2000 chars for debugging
51	
52	            # Extract ad text from each card
53	            for i, card in enumerate(ad_cards[:10]):  # limit to 10
54	                try:
55	                    text = card.inner_text()
56	                    results["active_ads"].append({
57	                        "index": i,
58	                        "text": text[:500]
59	                    })
60	                except:
61	                    pass
62	
63	            # If no cards found via selector, parse text for ad indicators
64	            if results["ad_count"] == 0:
65	                # Look for patterns in page text
66	                if "No ads found" in content:
67	                    results["note"] = "No active ads found for this dealer"
68	                elif "Active" in content and ("results" in content.lower() or "ads" in content.lower()):
69	                    results["note"] = "Ads may be present but selectors need updating — check screenshot"
70	                else:
71	                    results["note"] = "Page loaded but no ad data extracted — check screenshot"
72	
73	    except Exception as e:
74	        results["error"] = str(e)
75	
76	    if output_file:
77	        with open(output_file, "w") as f:
78	            json.dump(results, f, indent=2)
79	        print(f"Saved to {output_file}")
80	    else:
81	        print(json.dumps(results, indent=2))
82	
83	    return results
84	
85	
86	if __name__ == "__main__":
87	    dealer = sys.argv[1] if len(sys.argv) > 1 else "Brian Harris BMW"
88	    output = sys.argv[2] if len(sys.argv) > 2 else None
89	    scrape_meta_ads(dealer, output)
90
